chapter_title.en: Chapter on Bequests for Manumission, Charity, and Hajj

text: 5501 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَوْصَى بِثَلاَثِينَ دِينَاراً يُعْتَقُ بِهَا رَجُلٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا فَلَمْ يُوجَدْ بِذَلِكَ قَالَ "يُشْتَرَى مِنَ اَلنَّاسِ فَيُعْتَقُ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5501 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Ali ibn Abi Hamzah who said:
I asked Abu Al-Hasan (as) about a man who bequeathed thirty dinar for the emancipation of a man from among our companions, but no such person was found.
Imam (as) said: "A person should be purchased from among the people and then emancipated."
